Undid revision [[Special:Diff/1326830173|1326830173]] by [[Special:Contributions/OmegaAOL|OmegaAOL]] ([[User talk:OmegaAOL|talk]]) are there "varying degrees" of occupation under IHL? not sure about that. maybe worth a talk page clarification.

The International Court of Justice is the top court of the ''United Nations''. My point is not that Gaza is not fully occupied now (it is), but that consensus on that position has not been consistent since 2023, which is why "varying".

This is not merely the UN position, it is the consensus view in international law, including the ICJ (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cjerjzxlpvdo) [[Special:Diff/1326829274|1326829274]] by [[Special:Contributions/OmegaAOL|OmegaAOL]] ([[User talk:OmegaAOL|talk]])
The UN is not the final authority on such matter(s). At several points between the start of the war and today, scholarly consensus has stated that the Gaza territory was not completely occupied.

/* History */  Mostly minor spelling or grammatical corrections; The word "its" is an exception to the usual rule about apostrophes. Hence, even though it is possessive, it has no apostrophe, since someone decided that "it's" (with an apostrophe) is [a spelling that is used ONLY for] the contraction for "it is". Obviously it takes TWO (persons, or other "senior" items) in order to be "the two most senior" of anything. If there were several Israeli armies, then "the" article would have been "an".
